reviews.review_msg: I really enjoyed this class. The midterm was open book, but I found it more difficult than the final exam. The assignments were fair. Attendance was mandatory. I'd definitely take another class with this prof. Super nice, easy going, and a good teacher.

Rather disappointed because the final was beyond anything that had been expected, even though I studied hard for it. The midterm was open-book and I think the grades must have been low because the final was closed and the questions asked were bizarre; terms that hadn't been used, questions that required the films to be viewed far more than once.

Great, enthusiastic prof who explains concepts clearly while giving you the freedom to include your own thoughts instead of regurgitating lecture notes. Screens a wide variety of films, both mainstream and other, providing a unique and stimulating experience. If I have one complaint, it's in FPA 137, and the lazy essay format of the exams.

Clear, articulate and enthusiastic about course matter. Only 3 assignments and only the final paper required a significant effort/time investment. You may find some of his selections to be challenging or difficult to watch. Remember you are here to be challenged, not entertained.

Don't buy the textbook; there's no final exam for the course. Don't expect to see 'great' films in this class. Blair's approachable and intelligent but I'm very upset about him showing Crash (1996) right after course evaluations. It's practically****; if you don't believe me, look it up. It has a rating worse than R and should never be shown.

Blair is one of my favourite profs to date. He has a humourous, casual style of lecturing that keeps most of the class engaged and interested. He shows a great selection of films and introduces a lot of ideas I never knew prior to taking the class. I wish he taught more classes, or I could take this class again.
No Comments
No Comments

Blair is a good professor. My biggest problem with him is that he talks way too fast, and he chooses rather boring films. I fell asleep in all the lectures because the films never really held my attention, with the exception of "Ali: Fear Eats the Soul," I liked that a lot. Also, the textbook he uses is very very boring and irrelevant.

Not only the coolest dude in the school, but one helluva nice guy, looks like Edward Norton in Fight Club. If you can handle writing essays that say alot about nothing, then this course is gravy. Watch the movies, have opinions...that's it!
Awesome course, informable content. Good use of film choice.  A fair marker.
If you go into this class not knowing anything about film, be prepared to be overwhelmed.  It's a lot of work for a beginner, but certainly do-able.  And quite interesting as well.

Blair has a way of speaking - he puts an emphasis on weird words - kind of like Chandler from Friends, and it gets really annoying and hard to listen to during his lectures. Also, he doesn't seem to really care what we think about the films. Instead he seems to be saying, "this film is good because ____" as though a viewer's opinion is unimportant.

boo-urns.  high hopes for this class.  but if you aren't a film buff then be prepared to become overwhelmed.  and blair's chill teaching style makes it slightly difficult for people who aren't planning to become the next stanley kubrick.  over all...meh.  good experience, but completely replaceable.
^^word

Great prof, always willing to help. All the negative ratings below seem like the same person with some grudge, and thats just SAD. I came into this course expecting not to like it (because I needed a fine arts course for my program), but Blair did such a great job that I ended up loving the class, as did everyone else I talked to.

I had Blair as my TA/english prof last semester and I greatly enjoyed his classes. He's very much into what he's tought us and what he's teaching us this semester. He's fair and he encourages us to share our ideas and opinions. If FPA 135 wasn't one of my core courses this semester, I would have taken it with no regrets!

Blair is not yet ready to teach. He's a kid who tries to act like an adult & perhaps if he was just himself he'd teach much better. Instead he shows off, is ultra-formal and his class is really not for people who already have knowledge or ideas about films. I have lectured about film in the past at ivy league schools, so I KNOW it inside out... B-
No Comments
Pretty good, needs to learn to slow down and make sure everyone's on the same page, but he's understanding when you come and ask for help.

Information presented often not accurate. He does care about film, but not ready to teach. Opinions cloud his "facts". Films far to safe. Not as knowledgeable as he would like his students to think he is, but he is a fair marker.Papers too rooted in formal qualities. Be careful if you already have background knowledge in film and its history.

who wrote the entry before mine? Blair's arch nemesis!? Please...I'd like to see that person teach a class better. Blair is a fair, kind and knowledgable instructor with a real passion for what he is doing. He makes class fun and inspires us to learn more. If you have a chance to take a class with him, do it without hesitation!
Tries to overcompensate a LOT.  Doesn't REALLY get it.  I can't believe all those great comments.  He has potential, but he's just not nearly there yet.

Blair is amazing professor with a great sense of humour. Pay attention in class...it's not just a course where you can slack. A few students were surprised at how many assignments we had and how challenging they were. HOWEVER...listen, put in hard work and Blair is always there to help. He rocks!
great prof, i had  him for two semesters and it was a very fun class. He is very funny and very helpful. Would recommend this class to anyone.

yes blair is an awesome prof who knows his stuff! went in to fpa 137 knowing nothing about film and came out learning so much. LOVED IT! by far best course at sfu :) but the only bad thing.......... he does have a girlfriend. boo. hahaha..jk. TAKE BLAIRS CLASS!
BLAIR IS SO HOT RIGHT NOW.... SO HOT
Really good proff he knows his stuff.His classes are well structured and imformative. He is really approachable and always willing to help. He has a great personality and is also a cuttie:)

He was a fabulous prof.  Always very helpful and was very clear that he was knowledged in what he was teaching.  He was passionate about his class and tried his best to help the class understand the material.  Most films were very interesting. I thought it was a class worth taking as an elective.  M

Pretty cool prof! Very approachable, funny, and easy going. It is nice to go to a class where the teacher is interested in and passionate about what hes teaching. I really enjoyed his class. Pretty cute too!

This is one great professor! Very approachable, friendly, knowledgeabe and easy to listen to. He really seems to love what he's doing-but be careful of his writing-it's kind of hard to decipher. He's definately the best professor I've ever had. He's funny and smart...plus, he's not hard on the eyes!
Super helpful guy. Always in a good mood. Anyone who thinks Team America was hilarious is a-ok.

Really nice guy, and very approachable. Lectures may seem long at times but he'll crack the odd joke or do an impression to lighten the mood. Keep in mind time period of movies you're watching b4 signing up.My advice is not to leave the big essay until the last minute...o crap i'd better go do mine
Nice guy, good sense of humour. Anyone who talks about the Simpsons in class is okay by me.  I enjoyed this class more than I thought I would.
No Comments
